other_observers: Steve Mlodinow, Doug Faulkner, Bill Schmoker, Maggie Boswell, John Vanderpoel, Bill Kaempfer.; Steve Mlodinow found this bird.
description: This was a large tern, larger than nearby Ring-billed Gulls and nearly as large as nearby California Gulls.  It was considerably larger than the commonly seen Forster's and Common Terns. The head had a black stripe running from just behind the eyes round the back of the head. The forehead and the top of the head were white. The bill was orange/light red and was not too long and quite thick, especially near the base. The back and upper wings were light gray, except for the primary tips, which were rather dark and blackish. The underparts and both upper and underside of the tail were pure white. Leg color was quite difficult to see, but was certainly dark. I didn't see the bird nearly as close as the photo uploaded.Behaviors: Sitting on an island in the middle of a large plains reservoir. I also observed it flying around and plunge diving in typical tern fashion: diving into the surface, but not deep at all because it would take off rather quickly and resume flying around.Call: None heardPlumage: Basic
